steps.title: Target estate adjustment
steps.content: The claim is that Professor Serebrakoff cannot adjust to living at the country estate. The strongest evidence should show the professor himself contrasting estate life with the library and lecture hall he is used to, then expressing dissatisfaction with his current surroundings.

steps.title: Eliminate weaker evidence
steps.content: Choice A gives Vanya's disillusionment with the professor's career, not the professor's own adjustment. Choice B shows general self-loathing but not estate-specific discomfort. Choice C shows unfamiliarity with practical affairs and reliance on others, not rejection of country estate life. The quotation about being plunged into a wilderness and condemned to the estate directly supports the claim. The answer is D.
